summary.intent: Fix a legitimate bug in the `html_to_text()` function (multibyte/non-ASCII character handling causing panics) identified in PR #450, but replace the PR's O(n²) fix with a proper solution using an existing crate.
summary.outcome: Replaced the ~100-line hand-rolled HTML parser with the `html2text` crate (0.16.7), reducing the function to 8 lines, fixing all multibyte issues, and adding 4 regression tests. All 46 web_fetch tests pass. Changes were committed, pushed, and a PR was created linked to the original issue.

repo: Workspace dependencies are defined in the root Cargo.toml under [workspace.dependencies] with a 'Content processing' section around line 237.
CLAUDE.md enforces a rule against using `.unwrap()` in production code — error handling must use proper fallbacks.
The project uses conventional commits style based on recent commit history check before committing.
No Rust HTML-to-text crate was previously used; only references to Python html2text existed in sandbox packages.

code.finding: The original html_to_text() used raw byte indexing (`bytes[i] as char` and `&html[i..]`) which panics on multibyte/non-ASCII characters and corrupts output. Was ~100 lines with only 6 hardcoded entity decodings.
Dependencies are workspace-referenced; adding a new crate requires updating both the root Cargo.toml workspace deps and the individual crate's Cargo.toml.

workflow: The html2text crate returns a Result, requiring error handling before the function signature could remain infallible (used fallback to raw string on error).
Running targeted tests (`cargo test html_to_text`) before running the full crate test suite (`cargo test -p tools`) is an efficient verification workflow.
Checking for compilation warnings as a final step (`cargo check`) caught no issues but confirmed a clean build.

friction: Multiple grep attempts were needed to locate workspace dependencies — the pattern matching for `[workspace.dependencies]` required several tries with different escaping.
The html2text crate API returns a Result rather than a plain String, requiring an additional edit pass after the initial replacement failed tests.
Three separate Bash calls were needed to retrieve PR #450 details before the diff was successfully retrieved.
open_items: The PR created has not been explicitly linked to the original issue #450 in the transcript — the link step was requested but the transcript ends at the commit stage without confirming the PR URL or issue link.

The html2text crate's text formatting (line wrapping, whitespace handling) may differ from the original hand-rolled parser in edge cases not covered by the 8 tests — real-world HTML fetching could surface behavioral differences.
Only 6 hardcoded HTML entities were handled by the original function; the crate handles all entities, but no tests were added for the expanded entity coverage beyond the 4 multibyte regression tests.
